Hello and welcome to Hybrid Fulfillment. Let's get jiggy with it. So, before we even talk about fulfillment, we gotta define what it is. As I am defining it for the purposes of this course, training, et cetera, everything that happens between you making the first sale and the next sale, including but not limited to delivering on promises and over delivering to increase the likelihood that you'll make that next sale a reality, all right? So that is what we're trying to do here, is obviously now that we've, this person has gone from prospect to customer, now we have to deliver on our promises and totally blow them away so that they want to continue to stay and continue to pay and refer friends and just love us, okay? So, the intention behind what we have created here that we're gonna go over in the remainder of the trainings is was to create a program construct that one, 90% of general population would be able to, two, actually follow and adhere to, to three, see sustainable long-term results. So, four, we could create something that was superior to purely online or purely in-person approach, inclined outcomes, revenue generation, and lifetime value. And so, there's a lot of stuff in those four statements that those are very loaded. So, everything we did here is for, is general population. Since that's the majority of the customers that we have, we could have created an incredibly advanced programming, et cetera thing that no one would be able to follow and would likely not stick with, right? We are not our customers. And so, we have to create something that is easy for them to follow that they can actually adhere to, gain confidence with, so that they can change minimal things and their behaviors so that they can have maximum outcomes over the long-term. When I'm talking long-term, I'm not talking six months, 12 months, I'm talking about years and the rest of their life. And so, that is why you will hear me talk about things more like exercise and activity, more so than training and workouts. Because what we're shooting for is to get someone to the point where they actually could train, right? But there's no point in creating complex programming for somebody who doesn't even understand how to move or doesn't even move at all. It's just putting the cart before the horse and it doesn't set them up for success. And so, Dr. Gashy and I put a ton of time into thinking, what are the minimal things that we could get someone to do to create massive success because we would have such a low fallout rate. And that's probably that leads into that fourth point which is we can increase the amount of lifetime value of the customer and increase revenue generation and increase their client outcomes purely from the fact that they don't fall off because they're able to succeed because the program is structured such that succeeding is not difficult or cumbersome or sacrificing on their behalf, okay? And so, I just like to reiterate this since I know people have beliefs around this and people are very dogmatic about fitness and nutrition and whatnot. No one has helped if they only eat healthy or work out hard for six weeks or even six months only to stop and or regain the weight. No one's helped. We don't really help anyone, right? And they're only truly helped more so if they can do it for years and have sustainable habits, knowledge and confidence to keep it off. So they have the tools and the confidence to execute them, right? This is the holy grail and this is what we are providing and this is what we are selling, all right? And so, the duration of the fulfillment where we have it structured is 24 weeks and it's a repeatable cycle, all right? And that's what's great about this is you consistently can repeat the cycle over and over again, all right? And so, the outcome here is that we have a self-sufficient coachable client who can control and is aware of their food intake, how to adjust it relative to their goals and how to execute a fitness program also right for their goals and level, all right? So, disclaimer, you are an adult and a fitness professional with a brain. It is my assumption that you will use it. No shit. If someone requires an adjustment to this base level of fulfillment, by all means, do so. That is the essence of personalization and coaching, right? Example, an in-shape girl wants to go to the next level. Start her further along in the process wherever she is at a higher level. Note, most people think they're more advanced than they really are. Most people think they know what they're doing and then when you actually give them something to track, then they freak out. So, most times you will be best suited starting someone at the beginning and taking them through the process even if they feel like they're advanced, all right? A process can be personalized. So if the question of, well, if we're creating a 24-week template, how is that personalized? It is a process. The personalization comes to what we're doing with the individual during that process, but the process remains the same so we can create a consistent experience and a consistent outcome across all clients, all right? Second disclaimer, I just claim any liability. We do, related to health-related nonsense. We are not doctors nor do we present ourselves as such, nor should you, unless you are a doctor. We do not treat diseases and you probably don't either. So don't pretend to, all right? We leave liability-related anything you advise or imply with your clients. You're providing lifestyle recommendations. Our client should check with a physician for engaging in exercise and diet regimen, need help. All right, so there's four components to how we set this up for fitness, nutrition, supplementation and accountability, which is a.k.a. coaching and communication cadence, all right? So fitness-wise, in this 24-week plan, where the goal is to get somewhere from inactive to training and or exercising in a way that they can do forever that is aligned with their goal and their skill level, okay? Point B, in a gradual manner, such that they do not fall off, all right? That's a key point, is that we want to do it so that they cannot fail, right? Or it makes it extremely unlikely that they fail so that we can get them these win after win after win after win and help them gain momentum so that they gain the confidence that maybe someday they can actually really go to a gym on their own knowing what they're gonna do and feel excited about going there, rather than feeling insecure and not good enough. And so ultimately, they fall back off again, all right? That is the goal with the fitness program that we have. It is your prerogative to add as much complexity as you desire, but this is the recommendations that we have for 90% of general population who are gonna be coming into this for the goal of trying to lose weight and trying to be healthy, okay? And we go into more depth on that later, but once you have someone who is a coachable client, that is when you can have fun. That is when you can add in the more complex programming if you so desire, or you can add fun goals like a 5K, a marathon, a pull-up, mobility. It depends on who they are and what they really want. And so then you can program things in that way so that they can have those ancillary benefits as well that come along with the habits and the baseline habits that they're creating right now. These people are not games athletes. It is your mom and my mom who just wanna be healthy. Reminder. Okay, so if you wanna create all this stuff, I just said this, but I would insist that if it's normal person in general population, it is overkill to try and create programming for every single one of them when they are just wanting to be healthy and filled in. All right, second point, nutrition. The goal is to get some from unconscious eating to fully aware controlled eater, all right? That is what we're trying, that is the transformation that we're trying to do from a nutrition standpoint. And we're trying to do this in a gradual way so not to overwhelm a new person or make it unrealistic that they can sustain it. A lot of people can do a protein spray amount of a fast in the beginning, which is essentially just eating protein and starve themselves, but it is not sustainable in the long term for like very likely, right? They'll lose weight, but they'll probably get it back. All right, three is that the way that the structure does a full cycle of weight loss all the way back to sustained weight. So we're actually going to take them into deficit, get them to lose weight and then continue to feed them up, still in a deficit, so they're still losing fat up to the point of maintenance. And the goal is to get them to a maintenance level that is the same as they were before or higher if at all possible, right? And that is, when we do this properly, you can get people to even sustain it, a higher clerk can take them they were when they began, all right, and that is how we can get people to permanent weight loss. And then once you get to that point, you can begin the next cut and do it again, all right? Now, we also structured this so that we'd have quick short-term early weight loss wins because research-wise, it increases adherence and buy into the process. So when people lose weight in the beginning, they buy into the process and you gain trust, which increases adherence later down the road and increases their trust in you as a practitioner, which is incredibly important, all right? So that is the goal of nutrition and Trevor outlines the nitty gritty of each one of these things so that you can see how they progress throughout this period of time so that at the end, you can actually make someone a macros plan and you can do more fancy things and you can front load and back load and do carbs and cycle things in and out because you have that ability because that person is now coachable and it has the skill set to execute. If you tell GenPop to count macros out of the gate, they will likely fail, reminder. Supplementation, all right? So through this process, we tried to pair optimal supplementation for each phase of the process, all right? And so the first phase you can see is dedicated fat loss, all right, that's for the first eight weeks. The next is fat loss and performance and then the third is recovery, all right? And so the four pack of choice will be whatever is optimal for that specific cycle, that phase in the process, all right? And we do this to increase buying, increase investment and increase results of the client. We also do this to increase adherence because people who pair strategy with something that they're paying for are more likely to do it, all right? And it also increases revenue per customer and subscription lifetime value both in the service side and in the product side, all right? And so I'm just gonna show you this little slide that I got that I thought really drives this point home because we as practitioners must use every tool in our arsenal to increase motivation and adherence. So here's a post from Joel Danford. He said, hey, wanted to add some value. I think the post may really head to head for supplement, check it out. It was a precursor to the winner post. I even had a lady sign up for $200 worth based on the stats that I presented. So he says, hey, tribe, you know, the number is 4,280 challenger and here's what we have so far. Out of 70 starters, we had 40 finishers. Out of 40 finishers, 25 used prestige lab supplements. Total weight loss from 40 finishers was 215 pounds. Woohoo, total body fat loss from 40 was 257% of body fat percentage, which is total, which is great. And then five overall stats, basement averages are as follows. One, average weight loss without prestige labs was 3.1 pounds in four weeks. Average weight loss with prestige labs was seven pounds in four weeks. All right, more than double. Now, I'm not saying that the supplements were the reason that the science and all the formulations were the thing that doubled the fallows. I'm simply acknowledging the fact that people who purchase supplements in this example were able to lose more weight. And that is because you could attribute to whatever you want, right? Obviously, there's something to do with the stuff that's inside of the product, but I, even being the person who owned the company, right, who owns the company, would say that it's probably far more likely because they were more invested in the process, right? And so our goal as practitioners is to use every single tool in our advantage to increase motivation and increase adherence so we can increase client outcomes. And so it is in their best interest that we make the recommendations that we do so that they can have the best outcomes, all right? You can even see the total, the body fat loss without was 6.9% overall, and then the body fat loss with the prestigious lab supplements was 8.2, all right? So a significant difference there. And so this is something that I just like to use to drive this point home, all right? And so the last of the four pieces here is the accountability portion, which is kind of the communication cadence. This is a summary. There's a more detailed explanation of each one of these things, but I thought it would prove us to dig into this, all right? So client inbound, this is what the client is responsible for from a communication standpoint to you. One is daily weight check-ins. That is what they're going to do for you, and they're gonna do that via Voxer, all right? Or whatever software we have prescribed, but for right now, it's Voxer, all right? B, bi-weekly in-person meetings that are 15 minutes long, all right? And so that means that they are checking in every day, and we do that to increase engagement and keep them reminding every day that they are, that that is what they're supposed to be thinking about. The first thing every day when they start the day is how they're going to be healthy, and that single thing, just by having people weigh in every day, the Hawthorne effect takes hold, and the likelihood that they make positive choices kicks in, okay? So that's all they're responsible for, and we make this easy as heck for them, because from a communication standpoint, most people are lazy, and if you have a really rigorous communication cycle, it's very likely they're gonna fall off because they just don't wanna do it, right? Because they get lazy, whatever. All right, now for the coach, we also try to make this optimal for the coach so that they didn't have tons and tons of stuff that they had to do, all right? And so they will have a Monday, Wednesday, Friday, or Tuesday, Thursday response cadence. So you're gonna get a message every day, but every other day, which you will segment your clients into Monday, Wednesday, Friday, Tuesday, Thursday, or Saturday, which you can or cannot tell them, it's up to you, you will answer with a question that is in response to either a fitness question or a nutrition question or a mindset type question on each of those three days. That way we can pulse check on different aspects of the client's life, all right? And then bi-weekly, in the in-person meeting, that is where we discuss the changes and the improvements of the plans. And so the cadence that we've set up is that every other week, every 14 days is a new thing that we are gonna be introducing, new component to either the fitness, nutrition, the supplementation, so that as each phase moves on, we can introduce those in-person so that there's absolute clarity so they understand what is going on, and you don't have to try and write massive paragraphs or 20-minute-long voxers, but simply explain it to the in-person, answer their questions so they feel very clear, and they can walk out the door knowing exactly what they need to do, all right? And then the in-between communication is going to be missing aliniest messages and questions on voxers, where they have questions that come up, which is normal. The second is the Facebook group, where they will post larger questions, which you can also direct people to do to go there so that you can answer questions publicly, which is fine. And then when you have events at your facility, because if you were doing hybrid the way that we intended, the majority of the people are gonna be local within reason, so they might come to your facility for events or workshops that you sponsor to increase engagement in the community and also encourage referrals. So, let's dive in.
